question_text: According to the particle model, what causes the pressure of a gas?

options.label: A
options.text: The weight of the gas pushing downwards

options.label: B
options.text: The particles colliding with the walls of the container

options.label: C
options.text: The colour of the gas molecules

options.label: D
options.text: The temperature of the container walls

correct_answer: The particles colliding with the walls of the container
explanation: According to the particle model, gas particles are constantly moving at high speed. Gas pressure is created when billions of these particles collide with the container walls every second. Each tiny collision pushes on the wall, and all these collisions together produce the pressure we measure.
